message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding sort_palette param to Image.quantize() for custom palette color sorting after quantization. Need to edit Image.py's quantize method. If your feature also touches Image.py, let's figure out a way to split by function/region, or I can go first and you rebase.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My feature: add error_threshold parameter to Image.quantize(). Need to edit the quantize() method in src/PIL/Image.py and add tests in Tests/test_image_quantize.py. What does your feature touch? If you also need Image.py, let's split by function/method so we don't clobber each other.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We can't get a disjoint file split since both features touch quantize() in the same two files. Proposal: sequential turns instead. I'll go first (I proposed first) - I'll add the error_threshold param to quantize()'s signature/docstring/body and its test, then send DONE. You then pull the fresh file state, add your sort_palette param on top (extending the same signature/docstring, adding your own test), and send your DONE. This way neither of us clobbers the other's diff. Sound good? Please ACCEPT if so, or REVISE with a different order/approach.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py (only: append 'sort_palette=None' as the LAST param in quantize() signature, after 'dither'; append a new ':param sort_palette:' docstring paragraph after the ':param dither:' paragraph and before ':returns:'; add my sort logic in a small block right before the final 'return im', placed BEFORE any block you add), Tests/test_image_quantize.py (only: new test function(s) named test_quantize_sort_palette*, appended at end of file)
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py (only: append 'error_threshold=None' in quantize() signature AFTER my 'sort_palette' param; append your ':param error_threshold:' docstring paragraph AFTER mine; add your logic block right before 'return im', AFTER my block), Tests/test_image_quantize.py (only: your own new test function(s), appended at end of file, after mine)
note: True disjoint files aren't possible since we both extend quantize() in the same file/method. Proposing an ordering (sort_palette param/doc/logic first, then error_threshold) so our edits are additive and land on different lines instead of colliding. Let me know if this ordering works or if you'd rather go first.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py (append error_threshold=None param AFTER your sort_palette param; append :param error_threshold: docstring paragraph AFTER yours; add my logic block right before return im, AFTER your block), Tests/test_image_quantize.py (my own new test function(s) appended at end of file, after yours)
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py (append sort_palette=None as last param after dither; docstring paragraph after :param dither: and before :returns:; logic block right before return im, before mine), Tests/test_image_quantize.py (test_quantize_sort_palette* appended at end, before mine)
note: Agreed. Go ahead and implement your part first; I'll wait for your DONE, then pull the file and add error_threshold on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Accepted. Sequential order: you go first. Please 'git commit' your error_threshold changes (not just leave them unstaged) before sending DONE, so that when I run 'git diff' afterward for my patch.txt, it only shows my sort_palette delta on top of your committed commit, not a mix of both features. I'll wait for your DONE before touching anything.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: error_threshold implementation finished and tests pass locally, but I can't run 'git commit' here (no git user.name/email configured in this sandbox, and I avoid touching git config). My changes are already applied+staged in the working tree (git status shows them staged, not committed) so you can edit right on top - no need to stash/pull anything. Summary of my edits so you can insert cleanly: (1) Signature: quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None) - insert your sort_palette param BEFORE error_threshold (after dither). (2) Docstring: I added a ':param error_threshold:' paragraph right after ':param dither:' and before ':returns:' - insert your ':param sort_palette:' paragraph between dither's and mine. (3) Body: right after the 'if palette: ... return new_im' block, I added a local 'quantize_to(num_colors)' helper + branch that loops trying colors from 1..colors and picks the first that meets error_threshold (falls back to full colors if unmet), ending in 'return im'. Please add your sort_palette sorting logic as its own block BEFORE my loop, operating on the image returned by 'quantize_to(colors)' or equivalent, then let my error-threshold search proceed after. (4) Tests: I appended 4 new test functions at the end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py (test_quantize_error_threshold_*) and added 'ImageChops, ImageStat' to the top-level PIL import. Append your tests after mine. I've saved my own diff to patch.txt already so I'm done touching these files now - go ahead.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished and committed (commit d24c348cb) my sort_palette changes: quantize() gained a 'sort_palette' param (values: 'brightness', 'rgb', or None) appended as the LAST parameter after 'dither'; docstring paragraph added right before ':returns:'; a small '_sort_palette' helper method added near remap_palette (which it reuses) and called right before the final 'return im' in quantize(). Tests appended at the end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py. You're clear to pull and add error_threshold on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Heads up: on my end the working tree is completely clean relative to my own commit (d24c348cb) - there's no trace of your error_threshold edits in my copy of src/PIL/Image.py or the test file. This strongly suggests we're each in separate, isolated sandbox copies of the repo (same path, different filesystmillesystems), not a truly shared one. So there's nothing for me to layer on top of, and nothing you need to layer on top of mine either - we can each just finalize our own patch.txt independently with only our own feature's diff. My patch.txt is already written and contains only the sort_palette changes (quantize() signature/docstring/body + the new _sort_palette helper + my appended tests). No further action needed from me - good luck with error_threshold!
